title: wire credibility multiplier into recall scoring (plan-context.ts:1528 + mirror :1585)
description: Add a credibilityFactor(item, context) helper and multiply the fused recall score by it at the single insertion point plan-context.ts:1528, then mirror it EXACTLY in scoreBreakdownForItem (:1585) so score_breakdown.credibility is emitted and final === score. The factor is a content-age exponential decay off created_at, per-type half-life (from readCredibilityHalfLives), clamped up by the per-maturity floor (from readCredibilityFloors). Orthogonal to recencyBoost (additive 7-day bump) — composes safely.
action: In packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts add `function credibilityFactor(item: RuleDescriptionIndexItem, context: ScoringContext): number` — parse item.description.created_at, compute ageDays = (context.nowMs - createdMs)/86400000, look up halfLife by item.description.knowledge_type (via context-resolved readCredibilityHalfLives), factor = 2^(-ageDays/halfLife), then floor = readCredibilityFloors[maturity], return Math.max(floor, Math.min(1, factor)); missing/unparseable created_at → return 1 (no penalty). At :1528 change `return content + structuralScaleFor(context)*structural + proximity;` to `return (content + structuralScaleFor(context)*structural + proximity) * credibilityFactor(item, context);`. At :1585 change `const final = bm25 + vector + salience + recency + locality + proximity;` to multiply the same subtotal by credibilityFactor and add `credibility` to the returned RecallScoreBreakdown. Thread the half-life/floor maps onto ScoringContext (resolved once at the planContext entry, not per-item disk reads). Run `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit`.

criteria: plan-context.ts:1528-area return is wrapped so the fused score is multiplied by credibilityFactor(item, context) (the return line contains '* credibilityFactor')
scoreBreakdownForItem (:1585-area) multiplies the same subtotal by the SAME credibilityFactor and the returned RecallScoreBreakdown object contains a `credibility` key
credibilityFactor returns 1 when created_at is missing or unparseable (no-penalty fallback, grep-visible early return)
credibilityFactor is clamped: result never below the per-maturity floor and never above 1 (Math.max(floor, Math.min(1, factor)) visible)

the `final === score` parity assertion in packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts (:210 + :528, `expect(entry.score_breakdown.final).toBe(entry.score)`) passes for entries carrying a credibility factor — add a fixture: a content-stale entry whose score_breakdown.final still equals its score after the multiplier is applied to BOTH paths
a recall.test.ts fixture asserts that, all else equal, a content-stale entry ranks strictly below a fresh entry (older created_at → lower score)
readCredibilityHalfLives/readCredibilityFloors are read ONCE per planContext call and threaded via ScoringContext, not re-read per candidate (no readFabricConfig inside credibilityFactor)
convergence.verification: node -e "const s=require('fs').readFileSync('packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts','utf8'); const okMul=(s.match(/\* credibilityFactor/g)||[]).length>=2; const okKey=/credibility[,:]/.test(s); process.exit(okMul&&okKey?0:1)"; pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- recall
convergence.definition_of_done: Recall ranking down-weights content-stale entries by a per-type/per-maturity-floored age factor, the wire score_breakdown reports the credibility component, and final still equals the ranking score by construction.

files.change: Add credibilityFactor helper (created_at age → 2^(-age/halfLife), floored by maturity, clamped [floor,1], default 1 on missing date). Multiply the fused return at :1528. Mirror in the breakdown final at :1585 and emit `credibility`. Add halfLives/floors maps to ScoringContext, resolved once at planContext entry.

implementation: Read scoreDescriptionItem (:1515), scoreBreakdownForItem (:1539), salienceScore (:1426), recencyBoost (:1480) and the ScoringContext type to confirm nowMs, description.created_at, knowledge_type, maturity are all reachable. Note scoreDescriptionItem/scoreBreakdownForItem are module-private — the parity guard is exercised end-to-end via recall.test.ts, not a direct unit call.

Add credibilityFactor(item, context): parse created_at (Date.parse); if not finite → return 1. Compute ageDays. Look up halfLife = context.credibilityHalfLives[knowledge_type] (fallback to a per-type default if type unset). factor = Math.pow(2, -ageDays/halfLife). floor = context.credibilityFloors[maturity ?? 'draft']. return Math.max(floor, Math.min(1, factor)).
Thread credibilityHalfLives + credibilityFloors onto ScoringContext; resolve them ONCE at the planContext entry via readCredibilityHalfLives/readCredibilityFloors (never inside the per-item helper).
At :1528 wrap the additive return in parentheses and multiply by credibilityFactor(item, context).
At :1585 compute the same subtotal, multiply by credibilityFactor(item, context) to get final, and add `credibility: credibilityFactor(item, context)` (compute once, reuse) to the returned object.
Update the RecallScoreBreakdown construction so the breakdown mirrors scoreDescriptionItem component-for-component (final = subtotal * factor).

Add a fixture to packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts (the `final===score` parity guard lives here at :210 + :528, since scoreDescriptionItem/scoreBreakdownForItem are module-private and cannot be called directly): assert a content-stale entry's score_breakdown.final still equals its score, and that it ranks below an otherwise-identical fresh entry.
Run `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- recall`.

success_metrics: recall.test.ts final===score parity holds for a credibility-carrying entry (:210/:528 assertions)
content-stale entry scores strictly below an otherwise-identical fresh entry

reference.pattern: maestro-flow scoring.ts:270 `score *= credibilityFactor`; existing final===score mirror discipline between scoreDescriptionItem and scoreBreakdownForItem

rationale.chosen_approach: Single insertion point (multiply the already-computed fused return) keeps the additive calibration untouched and localizes the change; mirroring in the breakdown preserves the by-construction final===score invariant. created_at is the only persisted age signal available (content_hash deferred).
decision_factors: multiplicative factor is orthogonal to the additive recencyBoost, so no double-penalty
per-maturity floor softens the 'content leads' invariant without ever zeroing a literal match
resolving config once per call keeps the per-candidate hot path allocation-free
rationale.tradeoffs: created_at reflects authoring time, not last content edit — an entry edited in place keeps its old created_at and decays as if unedited. Accepted for v1; the content_changed_at clock is the deferred fix.

risks: Mirror drift: editing :1528 but not :1585 (or vice versa) breaks final===score — the recall.test.ts :210/:528 parity assertion is the guard.
Applying credibilityFactor to a zero-content structural-only entry: multiplying near-zero by a floor stays small, ordering preserved — verify the structural-only case via recall.test.ts.
credibility?: number must already exist on the RecallScoreBreakdown interface (agents.ts) — landed by TASK-001; emitting it before TASK-001 completes fails tsc.
